comment: 50yo male, lifelong resident of rural area with documented private-well inorganic-arsenic contamination >50 µg/L (well above WHO + US EPA 10 µg/L guideline since 2006). Endemic high-arsenic groundwater region. Urinary inorganic arsenic elevated on screening. No personal arsenic-attributable malignancy diagnosis. Inorganic arsenic IARC Group 1 (Monograph 100C, 2012) — urothelial / bladder cancer, lung cancer (smoking-independent), non-melanoma skin (BCC + SCC + Bowen's). Latency 10-40y from exposure onset. Engine fires RF-ENVIRONMENTAL-ARSENIC-WATER-PREVENTION → routes to 2-track PreventionPlan: standard = water-source remediation (POU filtration / municipal connection / alternative source), urinalysis + cytology q1-2y for bladder surveillance, dermatologic exam annually, smoking-cessation reinforcement, lung-symptom awareness; aggressive = annual cystoscopy consideration + earlier LDCT. Validates v0.3 environmental pilot. Anchored DIS-UROTHELIAL + DIS-NSCLC + DIS-BCC.
